title: Settlement Receipt Schema v1
description: JSON Schema for cryptographic settlement receipts issued after authorized agent transactions. Receipts are independently verifiable W3C VCs that carry a reference to the delegation credential that authorized the transaction, enabling definitive dispute prevention. This is the first formal versioned receipt schema for the chargeback prevention flow.

type.description: Must include VerifiableCredential and one of: SettlementReceipt, TronTransactionReceipt, LightningPaymentReceipt

issuer.description: DID of the entity issuing this receipt

expirationDate.description: Receipt validity window (typically 7-30 days for verification purposes; the dispute-prevention value persists indefinitely)

id.description: DID of the agent that executed the transaction

counterparty_did.description: DID of the counterparty (e.g. AI Infra Co.)

transaction_hash.description: On-chain transaction hash (for TRON) or payment hash (for Lightning)

rail_specific.description: Rail-specific fields (TRON: sender/recipient addresses, token contract, confirmations, network; Lightning: preimage, bolt11, payee_node_pubkey)

authorization.description: Reference to the delegation credential that authorized this transaction. This is the field that makes dispute prevention work: the receipt cryptographically proves which human authorized this specific transaction at which level.

delegationCredentialId.description: URI of the delegation credential that authorized this transaction (e.g. 'urn:uuid:delegation-abc123')

authorizationLevel.description: The authorization level the human selected when approving

principal_did.description: DID of the human principal who issued the authorization

authorized_at.description: When the human approved the authorization

scope_summary.description: Human-readable summary of what was authorized (e.g. 'One-time purchase of $50.00 in API credits from NeuralBridge')

policy_version_hash.description: SHA-256 hash of the internal policy document that was active when authorization was granted. Required for Level 3 (policy) receipts; optional for Level 1 and Level 2. Included now as an optional field to avoid schema migration when Level 3 receipts are issued in production.

credentialSchema.description: Reference to the JSON Schema that validates this receipt credential

verificationMethod.description: DID URL of the signing key. Verifiers resolve this to the issuer's DID Document to obtain the public key.

proof.description: Ed25519Signature2020 proof. Verification requires only the receipt JSON and the issuer's published public key — no runtime dependency on AT's backend.
